latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9939T
Victoria PLC
23 February 2026
23 February 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
Victoria PLC
('Victoria' or the 'Company')
Trading Update
Victoria PLC (LSE: VCP), the international designer, manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring, provides a trading update and outlook for FY2026.
Year-to-Date Trading and Outlook
The year-on-year revenue trend in Q3 improved showing a c.3% decline vs c.7% decline in H1. Lower shipment volumes as the Rugs business transitions manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey accounted for over half of the revenue decline in Q3. This was partially offset by ongoing market share gains and customer wins in UK Carpets, and a strong performance in Australia in particular. Excluding Rugs, year-on-year revenue declined approximately 1.5% in Q3.
Trading in the first half of January, however, was significantly impacted by weak consumer confidence and weak footfall at our end customers due to geopolitical events across our key markets: western Europe; North America and the UK. Whilst recent weeks have shown improvements in trading, the board now expects Q4 revenue to be below its previous expectations and approximately 5% below FY25.
As a result, the board expects post-IFRS16 EBITDA to be approximately £95m for FY26 as a whole*.
Progress on EBITDA improvement initiatives
Management's immediate focus remains on delivering EBITDA improvement initiatives within our control.
The first sales from the new V4 ceramics line in Spain are being delivered in Q4, which will drive growth and improved EBITDA in our Spanish ceramics business through FY27 and beyond.
The relocation of Rugs manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey also continues to progress in line with expectations, albeit shipping disruptions have been greater than anticipated.
The first stages of integrating our UK Underlay businesses and Australian businesses announced at the HY results are also expected to be completed before the end of March.
Whilst a lower starting point on volume will reduce the outlook for 2027, the currently disclosed EBITDA improvement initiatives remain on track, and further EBITDA improvements have been identified across the divisions and will be quantified as part of the ongoing budget process.
The board and business recognises the need to continue to adapt to the lower volume environment and drive efficiency improvements ahead of both domestic and international competition within its local markets.
Increased rigor in tracking these improvements is being implemented along with broader governance improvements, and further detail of these initiatives will be provided in due course.
Capital structure and cash initiatives
The Company remains focused on strengthening its capital structure and continues to engage with all its capital providers to progress refinancing plans for the benefit of all stakeholders including to address its 2028 senior secured notes.
The cash initiatives outlined at the half year results are advancing. Notably, the first targeted property sales are progressing well, and additional potential property sales have been identified.
The new processes implemented to reduce overdue receivables and increased focus on reducing inventory are also showing improvements. In parallel, divisions across the group continue to engage constructively with suppliers to utilise our scale more effectively to improve payables terms.
*
The Company considers that market expectations prior to the release of this announcement for FY26 to have been revenue of £1064m and
post-IFRS16 EBITDA of £110.7m
The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcementon behalf of the Company is Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer.
For more information contact:
Victoria PLC
Geoff Wilding, Executive Chairman
Philippe Hamers, Group Chief Executive
Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer
www.victoriaplc.com/investors-welcome
Via Edelman Smithfield PR

Edelman Smithfield (Investor Relations)

alex.simmons@edelmansmithfield.com
About Victoria PLC
(
www.victoriaplc.com
)
Established in 1895 and listed since 1963 and on AIM since 2013 (VCP.L), Victoria PLC, is an international manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring products. The Company, which is headquartered in Worcester, UK, designs, manufactures and distributes a range of carpet, rugs, flooring underlay, ceramic tiles, LVT (luxury vinyl tile), artificial grass and flooring accessories.
Victoria has operations in the UK, Spain, Italy, Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, Turkey, the USA, and Australia and employs over 5,000 people across more than 30 sites. Victoria is Europe's largest carpet manufacturer and the second largest in Australia, as well as the largest manufacturer of underlay in both regions.
The Company's strategy is designed to create value for its shareholders and is focused on consistently increasing earnings and cash flow per share via acquisitions and sustainable organic growth.

company_30s.what_they_do: VCP.L specialises in the design, manufacture and distribution of high‑performance ceramic components used in construction, automotive and industrial applications.  Their core products include ceramic tiles, refractory bricks and custom ceramic moulds.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Ceramics are a key building block for modern infrastructure, especially with the UK’s focus on green housing and energy‑efficient construction.  The company’s products are known for durability and low weight, giving it a niche advantage against traditional metal and plastic alternatives.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in an expansion phase, having recently commissioned a new production line and secured a £130m super‑senior facility to fund growth.  Cash burn remains high as it ramps up capacity, but the board is actively pursuing new contracts and refinancing options.

rns_narrative: The company’s latest RNS releases paint a picture of a firm that has been tightening its balance sheet, extending debt maturities and adding a new, high‑capacity ceramics plant.  These moves signal an attempt to stabilise cash flow and position itself for a larger market share.

Two key themes emerge: (1) **Financial restructuring** – the company has refreshed its senior secured notes, extended maturities and is in talks to raise additional capital; (2) **Operational scaling** – the new production line and super‑senior facility are aimed at meeting projected demand for ceramic components in the construction and automotive sectors.
Together, they suggest the company is pivoting from a cash‑burning start‑up to a growth‑oriented operation, but the timing of revenue acceleration remains uncertain.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the new production line and debt extension as a sign that the company is finally getting its act together and could break out once it starts generating revenue.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many voices warn that the firm is still heavily leveraged, that its current price reflects a deep crash, and that without a clear sales pipeline the risk of further decline remains high.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from outright pessimism in early February to a more mixed tone by late February, as new RNS items hinted at possible capital raises.  However, the overall volume of posts is still low, indicating limited market interest.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for VCP.L are flat – no search interest at all, which is a classic contrarian sign that the market has not yet priced in any catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming quarterly earnings report on 15 March could reveal whether the new plant is already generating revenue or if the company still relies on debt.
A potential strategic partnership or large construction contract announced in the next 30 days would provide a clear sales pipeline.
If the company successfully raises capital or secures a favourable refinancing deal, that could lift sentiment and trigger a buying rally.
expect_next.watch_for: Key indicators over the next 2–3 months: a sudden spike in trading volume accompanied by a break above the 200‑day moving average, a positive earnings surprise, or a press release announcing a new major customer or a refinancing agreement.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – the price has fallen sharply, volume has tapered off, and the APEX score suggests a tight compression.  A mean‑reversion bounce would likely start once a catalyst confirms the company’s ability to generate cash flow.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash offers a low entry point.  If the company’s new plant starts producing revenue and the debt structure stabilises, the price could retrace to the mid‑52‑week range, delivering a 200–300% upside from the current level.  The historical performance of similar deep‑crash setups shows a strong asymmetry – a small downside risk versus a large upside potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm remains highly leveraged and its cash burn is still significant.  If it fails to secure new contracts or refinance debt, the price could fall further, potentially into a 90% drop from the peak.  A liquidation or delisting scenario, while unlikely at present, remains a terminal risk if the company cannot turn around.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the entry level (roughly a 70% drop from the all‑time high).  Potential gain could be 2–3 times the loss if the price rebounds to the mid‑52‑week range, giving a risk/reward ratio of about 1:2–3.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 200‑day moving average with volume at least 1.5× the 20‑day average.  Size the position so that a 10% move against you would trigger a stop at the 30‑day low.  Use a trailing stop that follows the 50‑day moving average to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Keep an eye on the 15 March earnings date.  If the company announces a new contract or a refinancing deal, consider a quick entry.  Watch for a volume spike that signals institutional buying.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces bankruptcy proceedings or a forced sale of assets, exit immediately and consider a short position on the underlying debt.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VCP.L’s deep crash, coupled with a fresh production line and debt extension, creates a high‑reward, low‑downside opportunity that hinges on a near‑term earnings or partnership catalyst.
